description: A device resets or misbehaves under load because inadequate decoupling lets the supply rail droop during current transients.
body: Failure mode: too little local capacitance or excessive mounting inductance leaves the rail unable to supply transient current, so the voltage sags below the device minimum. Detection signals: glitches that correlate with high-activity bursts, a rail dip on a scope during load steps, and improvement when bench capacitance is added. Do not raise the supply voltage to mask the droop. Safer fallback: add local high-frequency and bulk capacitance close to the pins with short connections and verify the rail under a load step.
